rows.llm_rationale: Crowd price of 9.5% appears slightly underpriced given base rates for Fed emergency cuts. With ~19 months remaining (to end-2026), probability of a financial crisis or severe economic shock severe enough to trigger emergency action is non-trivial (~12-15% range). CME FedWatch data likely reflects current stable conditions, but tail risks (banking stress, major recession) warrant modest uplift from crowd price.
rows.question: Fed emergency rate cut before 2027?

rows.llm_rationale: As of mid-May 2026, markets have priced in only ~16.5% odds of even 1 cut this year. Given typical Fed cycles and that we're 7+ months from year-end, this seems slightly underpriced if economic conditions soften or inflation continues moderating. CME FedWatch data historically suggests higher terminal odds for at least one cut over such a horizon. Adjusting modestly upward to ~22% accounts for baseline recession risk and policy optionality, while respecting the crowd's hawkish positioning.
rows.question: Will 1 Fed rate cut happen in 2026?
